overview.short: Kalpeshwar (Sanskrit: कल्पेश्वर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at an elevation of 2,200 m (7,217.8 ft) in the Urgam valley in the Garhwal region of Uttarakhand state
overview.description: Kalpeshwar (Sanskrit: कल्पेश्वर) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva located at an elevation of 2,200 m (7,217.8 ft) in the Urgam valley in the Garhwal region of Uttarakhand state in India. The temple's ancient legend linked to the Pandavas, heroes of the epic Mahabharata, is the fifth temple of the Panch Kedar (five temples) of Shiva's five anatomical divine forms; the other four temples in the order of their worship are Kedarnath, Rudranath, Tungnath and Madhyamaheshwar temples; all in the Kedar Khand region of the Garhwal Himalayas. topPlaces.name: Vishnuprayag Dam
topPlaces.description: The Vishnuprayag Dam lies in Chamoli district at the confluence of Alaknanda River and Dhauliganga River, in the Indian state of Uttarakhand, India.

items.note: The Vishnuprayag Dam lies in Chamoli district at the confluence of Alaknanda River and Dhauliganga River, in the Indian state of Uttarakhand, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kalpeshwar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kalpeshwar is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

The nearest airport is Dehradun Airport (Jolly Grant) (128 km) and the nearest railway station is Dehradun Railway Station (138 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kalpeshwar?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kalpeshwar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kalpeshwar?
faq.a: Stays in Kalpeshwar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kalpeshwar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vishnuprayag Dam.
